title: Two-column aggregation on join result extremely slow.
poc: CREATE TABLE t1 AS SELECT a1, a3, sum(prob) FROM tmp GROUP BY a1, a3 WITH DATA;
CREATE TABLE t1 AS SELECT a1, a3, sum(prob) FROM (SELECT ap.a1 as a1, bp.a1 AS a3, ap.prob * bp.prob as prob FROM ap, bp WHERE ap.a2 = bp.a2) AS tmp GROUP BY a1, a3 WITH DATA;
CREATE TABLE t1 AS SELECT a1, a3 FROM (SELECT ap.a1 as a1, bp.a1 AS a3, ap.prob * bp.prob as prob FROM ap, bp WHERE ap.a2 = bp.a2) AS tmp GROUP BY a1, a3 WITH DATA;
SELECT a1, a3, sum(prob) FROM tmp GROUP BY a1, a3 WITH DATA;
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE ap (a1 INT, a2 INT, prob FLOAT, PRIMARY KEY (a1, a2));
CREATE TABLE bp (a1 INT, a2 INT, prob FLOAT, PRIMARY KEY (a1, a2));
SELECT a1, a3, SUM(prob) FROM (SELECT ap.a1 AS a1, bp.a1 AS a3, ap.prob * bp.prob AS prob FROM ap JOIN bp ON ap.a2 = bp.a2) AS tmp GROUP BY a1, a3 WITH DATA;
